title: Who is eligible – Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit - Canada.ca
content:
Status: Starts July 2026
You may be eligible to receive the Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit (CGEB) and any related provincial or territorial programs, even if you have no income in the year.
If you are currently receiving the Canada child benefit (CCB) for a child, that child is automatically considered when the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) calculates your CGEB amount.

## Eligibility criteria
You are eligible for the benefit if you meet all of the following conditions:
- You are a resident of Canada for tax purposes during both periods: In the month before the CRA makes a payment At the start of the month when a payment is made Who is considered a resident of Canada You are considered to be a resident if you normally live in Canada and have established significant residential ties, for example if you have: A home in Canada A spouse or common-law partner in Canada Dependants in Canada If you are unsure about your residency status, refer to: Determining your residency status .
You are a resident of Canada for tax purposes during both periods:
- In the month before the CRA makes a payment
- At the start of the month when a payment is made
You are considered to be a resident if you normally live in Canada and have established significant residential ties, for example if you have:
- A spouse or common-law partner in Canada
- Dependants in Canada
If you are unsure about your residency status, refer to: Determining your residency status .
- You are at least 19 years old If you are turning 19 during the year If you have filed your tax return and meet all other eligibility criteria, the CRA will issue your first payment on the next payment date that comes after your 19th birthday. You are under 19 years old and meet at least one of the following conditions: You have (or had) a spouse or common-law-partner You are (or were) a parent and live (or lived) with your child
- You are at least 19 years old If you are turning 19 during the year If you have filed your tax return and meet all other eligibility criteria, the CRA will issue your first payment on the next payment date that comes after your 19th birthday.
You are at least 19 years old
If you have filed your tax return and meet all other eligibility criteria, the CRA will issue your first payment on the next payment date that comes after your 19th birthday.
- You are under 19 years old and meet at least one of the following conditions: You have (or had) a spouse or common-law-partner You are (or were) a parent and live (or lived) with your child
You are under 19 years old and meet at least one of the following conditions:
- You have (or had) a spouse or common-law-partner
- You are (or were) a parent and live (or lived) with your child

### You need all of the above to be eligible
To be eligible for the CGEB, you must meet all of the above criteria.
Based on your selections above:

### You may not meet all of the criteria
To be eligible for the CGEB, you must meet all of the above criteria.
Based on your selections above:

### You may be eligible for the benefit
You meet the criteria for the CGEB, however you must also check if you are entitled to receive the benefit based on your adjusted family net income level.
You may need to provide additional details to the CRA to confirm your eligibility.

## Income thresholds entitled to receive the benefit
You may be entitled to receive the CGEB if your adjusted family net income is less than the maximum income level thresholds listed below.
Check maximum income thresholds for:
Your adjusted family net income must be less than the maximum income thresholds to be entitled to receive the CGEB.
With 4 children or more
Your adjusted family net income must be less than the maximum income thresholds to be entitled to receive the CGEB.
With 4 children or more

## If you have shared custody
Parents in a shared custody situation may be eligible for half of the CGEB for that child. This also applies to any related provincial and territorial benefit programs.
If you are receiving the CCB for a child, that child is automatically included in your CGEB calculation. If you become primarily responsible for the care and upbringing of a child, your amounts of CGEB and related provincial or territorial benefit programs will increase based on your updated CCB information.
If a child is legally, physically, or financially under the responsibility of a child welfare agency, you are not generally eligible for the CGEB for that child.
Determine if your situation qualifies as shared custody for the CCB
If you have a spouse or common-law partner, your adjusted net incomes are combined to get your adjusted family net income (AFNI).

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
